comments.text: "who show it suffices to check $t\le c$" sounds confusing to me. trivial monotonicity show that $d(t,c)\le d(t,c+1)$. I presume the intended takeaway is that if $d(c,c)<\infty$, then $d(t,c)<\infty$ for all $t$.

From "Bounding the Chromatic Number of Dense Digraphs by Arc Neighborhoods(2024)" by F. Klingelhoefer and A. Newman, the problem is equivalent to asking: For all $c ≥ 0$, there exists $d ≥ 0$ such that if $T$ is a tournament with $\vec{χ}(T) ≥ d$, there are two sets $A, B ⊆ V (T)$ such that $\vec{χ}(T[A]), \vec{χ}(T[B]) ≥ c$ and all arcs between $A$ and $B$ go from vertices of $A$ to vertices of $B$. The above is essentially a conjecture from "Some results and problems on tournament structure(2023)" by T. Nguyen, A. Scott and P. Seymour.
